summary: Overlay a built-in kind's lock-declared `templates` (not just `governs`) so gate/explain actually fold nested-member fences on a templated built-in host.

edit.description: Generalize `effective_governs` (main.rs:769, governs-only today, one call site at `resolve_kind_units` main.rs:802) into one overlay that also lifts the matching lock row's `templates`. `row_relocates_builtin` (main.rs:997) already treats a non-empty declared `templates` as a legitimate relocation extension per its own doc comment (main.rs:993-995), but nothing consumes it. Thread the overlaid kind (not the bare `builtin_kind::definitions()` value) into every built-in extraction path: `gate`'s built-in dispatch loop (main.rs:577-593), `kind_features` (main.rs:845-854), and `builtin_features_by_kind` (main.rs:890-903) — one lookup, no second `effective_templates` function.

Add a case beside `check_walks_the_locks_declared_governs_locus_not_the_kinds_embedded_default`: a lock row named after a built-in (e.g. `rule`) declaring `templates = ["directive"]`, a rule member file containing a `member.directive <key>` fence, asserting the nested member is now folded and visible (currently dark regardless of fence content).

tests.asserts: a built-in host's lock-declared templates fold nested-member fences into gate/explain output instead of staying inert

acceptance: A lock row declaring `templates` against a built-in host (e.g. `rule` gaining a `directive` child kind via `withinHosts: ["rule"]`) has its member fences folded by `gate`/`explain`, matching what `from_kind_fact_row`'s own unit test (kind.rs:1034) already proves in isolation off the lock row alone.
notes: Routed from inbox T18 (observed 3ff0dd1, unblocked, reconfirmed unaffected by TEMPLATES-RELOCATION-COLLISION-REGRESSION and MEMORY-ENTERS-REQUIREMENT-CORPUS). Verified on disk this tick: from_kind_fact_row lifts row templates correctly (kind.rs:225-247); row_relocates_builtin's own doc comment (main.rs:993-995) already names templates a legitimate extension; nothing overlays it before extraction. No code changed since 3ff0dd1.

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels — SDK npm package, engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle — fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs the per-platform engine-binary release workflow build-out + John's decide-at-release calls (optional Apple notarization for the standalone mac binary, USPTO name screen) before the mechanical wrappers validate end-to-end. No marketplace/signing creds exist — a marketplace is a git repo + marketplace.json, community submission a free web form gated by `claude plugin validate` (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs); npm creds done 07-05. SDK scope resolved (`@dtmd/temper`, single package, `(sdk-package-layout)`, still at 0.0.5 pending publish — SDK-VERSION-LOCKSTEP); channel 1's SDK foundation shipped. Re-verified at dd7517a (ship audit): still no `.github/workflows/release.yml` (only `temper.yml`, a check job); root package.json is still the private flume manifest (`temper-flume-harness`).

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (distribution.md).

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, distribution.md). Reconcile with the flume manifest rather than clobbering it. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (`@dtmd/temper`, already published — the engine pin is declared there, once).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (distribution.md 'Fail-loud invariant').
notes: Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm `@dtmd/temper`; ch2 = engine binary via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human release creds + the engine-binary workflow. install.rs range-correctness shipped in SCAFFOLD-OUTPUT-VALID (^0.0.4); release owns only future re-pins.
